description_long: This is a large, multi-level industrial-style shelving unit constructed primarily from concrete and metal. The structure features four spacious horizontal shelves, each supported by a grid of robust vertical and horizontal beams, giving it a skeletal, open-frame appearance. The shelves are thick and solid, likely designed to bear heavy loads, and the open sides and back allow for easy access and visibility from multiple angles. The lower section is elevated on numerous sturdy legs, providing clearance beneath the bottom shelf. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible doors, handles, or decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. Its scale and construction suggest it is intended for use in environments such as warehouses, construction sites, or urban storage facilities.
description: A large, industrial open-frame shelving unit with four thick concrete shelves supported by a grid of metal beams and legs. The design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for heavy-duty urban storage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open grid structure and four solid shelves, with the leftmost section slightly recessed.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the shelves and the open, skeletal support beams beneath each level.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the uniformity of the shelves and the supporting legs, with no visible access panels or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full width of the shelving unit and the open framework beneath the shelves.
